title: Three InfoQ Certification Cohorts Start This August: Meet the Facilitators
gist: InfoQ is monetizing senior-engineer education through five-week paid certification cohorts at USD 1,470 each, notably adding an AI Security & Privacy track aimed at teams shipping AI in regulated industries.

themes.theme: Paid education as a media monetization play

insights.claim: InfoQ is running five-week online certification cohorts priced at USD 1,470 each, targeting practitioners with at least five years of experience and relying on employer professional-development reimbursement.
insights.speaker: Artenisa Chatziou
insights.so_what: Shows a developer-media brand extending into high-margin paid certification, a monetization path for content businesses facing ad pressure.
insights.quote: Each program costs USD 1,470 per cohort, and most companies reimburse professional development.
insights.locator: Each program costs USD 1,470 per cohort

insights.claim: InfoQ is launching at least five certification programs (Architecture, Engineering Leadership, AI Security & Privacy, plus AI Engineering and others), indicating a full product line rather than a one-off.
insights.speaker: Artenisa Chatziou
insights.so_what: Breadth suggests InfoQ views certification as a durable revenue segment worth watching for scale.
insights.locator: Full syllabi, pricing, and registration for all five programs

themes.theme: AI security in regulated industries as demand signal

insights.claim: An AI Security & Privacy Engineering cohort led by Katharine Jarmul is built specifically for teams shipping AI in regulated industries, covering sensitive-data handling, threat modeling, red teaming, sandboxes, evaluations, and governance/auditing.
insights.speaker: Artenisa Chatziou
insights.so_what: The existence of a dedicated regulated-AI security curriculum signals enterprise demand for AI compliance skills, an adjacent market for tooling vendors.
insights.quote: Her cohort is built for teams shipping AI in regulated industries
insights.locator: AI Security and Privacy Engineering, with Katharine Jarmul

insights.claim: The AI Security cohort's capstone is a documented risk assessment and mitigation report for an AI product architecture, reflecting that formal AI risk documentation is becoming an expected engineering deliverable.
insights.speaker: Artenisa Chatziou
insights.so_what: AI risk documentation as a standard artifact points to demand for governance/assessment tooling.
insights.locator: Participants work in small groups across the five weeks

insights.claim: The cohort format has senior engineers apply a framework from a QCon talk to a real work decision each week and debrief with a confidential peer group from other companies, a differentiated model targeting the isolation of the most-senior person in the room.
insights.speaker: Artenisa Chatziou
insights.so_what: Confidential cross-company peer cohorts are a moat against generic online courses; watch for retention and repeat enrollment.
insights.quote: often the most senior person in the room, with few outside voices to check their thinking
insights.locator: Senior engineers and architects making architecture
